content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 5,
1993
Honorable Ruben S.
Ayala
Senator,
34th District
State Capitol,
Room 2082
Sacramento,
California
95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-93—079
Dear Senator Ayala:
This is in response to your request for advice under the
Political Reform Act
(the ttActfl)
1 concerning your use of campaign
funds.
QUESTION
You desire to establish a scholarship at Ayala High School,
which is a public high school in your Senatorial district.
The
scholarship will be awarded for excellence in high school
academics and recipients of the scholarship will be selected by
the school’s administration.
The scholarship will be in your
family’s name.
May you use your campaign funds to fund this
scholarship?
CONCLUS ION
Your campaign funds may be used for this purpose,
so long as
they are donated to a bona fide educational organization and no
part of the donation will have a material financial effect on you,
your campaign treasurer,
any persons on your committee with
authority to approve the donation,
or any member of your immediate
family.
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000 et q.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A-93-079
Page
2
ANALYSIS
Section 89515 states:
Campaign funds may be used to make donations
or loans to bona fide charitable,
educational,
civic, religious,
or similar tax—exempt,
nonprofit organizations, where no substantial
part of the proceeds will have a material
financial effect on the candidate,
elected
officer,
campaign treasurer,
or any individual
or individuals with authority to approve the
expenditure of campaign funds held by a
committee,
or member of his or her immediate
family,
and where the donation or loan bears a
reasonable relation to a political,
legislative,
or governmental purpose.
On this basis,
a donation of your campaign funds to establish
a scholarship fund at a high school in your district would be
permitted if:
(1)
the funds are donated to the educational
institution or institutions through which the
scholarship would be awarded;
and
(2)
the donation cannot have a material
financial effect on you,
your campaign
treasurer,
any persons on your committee with
authority to approve the donation,
or any
member of your immediate family;
and
(3)
the donation is reasonably related to a
political,
legislative, or governmental
purpose.
This scholarship will be established at a high school in your
Senatorial district and will be in your family’s name.
Presumably,
establishment of the scholarship will result in
publicity for you.
Therefore, it appears that establishing the
scholarship fund would be reasonably related to a political
purpose.
Given these facts,
so long as you adhere to the
requirements set forth in subparts
(1)
and
(2)
in the paragraph
immediately above,
you would be permitted to use campaign funds to
establish this scholarship fund.


Our File No.
A-93-079
Page
3
Should you have additional questions,
please contact me at
(916)
322—5901.
Sincerely,
Jeff Marschner
General
ounsel
By,
cott Hallabrin
Senior Counsel,
Legal Division
J]4: SH:dl


California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 5,
1993
Honorable Ruben S.
Ayala
Senator,
34th District
State Capitol,
Room 2082
Sacramento,
California
95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-93-079
Dear Senator Ayala:
In response to your letter requesting advice dated
February
12,
1993,
enclosed is my staff’s response to and analysis
of your question.
If you have further questions,
please contact me or
Scott Hallabrin,
who authored the response letter.
Sincerely,
nDavidian

428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807

Sacramento, CA
95812
